articles.headline: RBI approves record dividend of ₹2.11 lakh crore for FY24
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India approved a surplus transfer of ₹2,10,874 crore to the Union Government for the 2023-24 fiscal year, a 141% increase over the previous year. The payout is significantly higher than the ₹1.02 lakh crore budgeted by the government. The central bank also decided to raise the contingency risk buffer to 6.5% to ensure economic stability.

articles.headline: Sensex rises 268 points tracking global trends and RBI news
articles.summary: Domestic equity benchmarks Sensex and Nifty ended higher on Wednesday, driven by gains in banking and financial stocks following the RBI's record dividend announcement. The BSE Sensex rose 267.75 points to close at 74,221.06, while the Nifty 50 climbed 68.75 points to 22,597.80. Positive cues from global markets also supported the overall investor sentiment.

articles.headline: Election Commission issues notice to parties over model code violations
articles.summary: The Election Commission of India has issued fresh directives to political parties to maintain decorum and avoid using religious sentiments during the ongoing Lok Sabha election campaign. The commission warned of strict action against candidates violating the Model Code of Conduct, following several complaints regarding provocative speeches and controversial advertisements.

articles.headline: Centre notifies new rules for direct listing on GIFT City
articles.summary: The Finance Ministry has notified the framework allowing Indian public companies to list their shares directly on international exchanges at GIFT City, Ahmedabad. This allows Indian firms better access to global capital markets through the NSE International Exchange and India International Exchange. The move aims to improve valuations for Indian startups and facilitate foreign investment.

articles.headline: Ireland, Spain, and Norway recognize Palestinian statehood
articles.summary: Ireland, Spain, and Norway have formally announced their decision to recognize a Palestinian state, aiming to support a two-state solution and lasting peace in the region. The move drew immediate praise from Palestinian leaders but was met with sharp condemnation from Israel, which responded by recalling its ambassadors from the three nations for urgent consultations.

articles.headline: UK inflation hits near three-year low of 2.3 percent in April
articles.summary: Consumer price inflation in the United Kingdom slowed to 2.3% in the year to April, down from 3.2% in March. This marks the lowest level since July 2021 and brings inflation closer to the Bank of England's 2% target. However, the drop was slightly less than economists' expectations, leading markets to scale back bets on an immediate interest rate cut in June.

articles.headline: Power demand hits all-time high of 233 GW amid heatwave
articles.summary: India's peak power demand reached a record high of 233 gigawatts (GW) on Tuesday as a searing heatwave gripped large parts of North and Central India. The Ministry of Power stated that necessary measures are being taken to ensure uninterrupted supply. The previous record was 224 GW set in September 2023, with increased air conditioning use being the primary driver.

articles.headline: Nykaa Q4 profit grows 187% to ₹6.9 crore
articles.summary: FSN E-Commerce (Nykaa) reported a 187% year-on-year jump in consolidated net profit to ₹6.9 crore for the March quarter. Revenue from operations increased by 28% to ₹1,668 crore. The growth was led by the Beauty and Personal Care segment, which saw strong demand during the summer sale. Gross Merchandise Value grew 32% across all platforms during the period.

articles.headline: Emami Q4 net profit rises 3.6% to ₹148.9 crore
articles.summary: FMCG major Emami reported a 3.62% increase in consolidated net profit to ₹148.9 crore for the fourth quarter ended March 2024. Revenue from operations grew 6.6% to ₹891.7 crore. The company saw volume growth of 6.2% in the domestic market, driven by healthcare and male grooming segments. The board also recommended a final dividend of ₹8 per equity share.

articles.headline: India's heatwave continues as temperatures cross 47 degrees in Rajasthan
articles.summary: Severe heatwave conditions persisted across North and Central India, with Barmer in Rajasthan recording a maximum temperature of 47.4 degrees Celsius. The IMD issued a 'red alert' for several states, advising citizens to avoid outdoor exposure during peak hours. The heatwave is expected to last for another three to four days before any significant relief arrives.

articles.headline: Gland Pharma reports ₹192 crore profit in March quarter
articles.summary: Gland Pharma posted a consolidated net profit of ₹192.4 crore for the quarter ended March 31, 2024, compared to ₹78.7 crore in the same period last year. Revenue from operations rose 96% year-on-year to ₹1,537.5 crore. The growth was attributed to the acquisition of Cenexi and strong performance in the base business across key markets including the US and Europe.

articles.headline: Adani Enterprises to consider maiden retail bond issue of ₹2,000 crore
articles.summary: Adani Enterprises is planning to raise up to ₹2,000 crore through its first-ever public issue of non-convertible debentures (NCDs) for retail investors. The funds are intended for capital expenditure and repayment of existing debt. This move signals the group's return to the public debt market as it seeks to diversify its funding sources and engage retail participants.

articles.headline: Government to sell 5% stake in NLC India via OFS
articles.summary: The Union government announced a plan to sell up to a 7% stake in NLC India through an Offer for Sale (OFS). The floor price has been set at ₹212 per share, representing a discount to the current market price. The sale is expected to help the government move towards its disinvestment targets for the current fiscal year while increasing public shareholding.

articles.headline: SEBI proposes easier norms for ease of doing business
articles.summary: The Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) proposed measures to simplify the regulatory framework for listed entities. Key proposals include providing more time for companies to fill vacancies in key managerial positions and simplifying the definition of 'social enterprises'. The move is part of an ongoing effort to facilitate ease of doing business for Indian firms.

articles.headline: KKR enters IPL 2024 final with dominant win over SRH
articles.summary: Kolkata Knight Riders (KKR) secured a place in the IPL 2024 final after an eight-wicket victory over Sunrisers Hyderabad (SRH) in Qualifier 1. Mitchell Starc’s three-wicket haul helped restrict SRH to 159, which KKR chased down easily with half-centuries from Venkatesh Iyer and Shreyas Iyer. SRH will now face the winner of the Eliminator in Qualifier 2 on Friday.

articles.headline: JSW Steel Q4 profit falls 64% on higher costs
articles.summary: JSW Steel reported a 64.5% year-on-year decline in consolidated net profit to ₹1,299 crore for the fourth quarter. The fall was attributed to lower steel realizations and higher raw material costs. However, revenue from operations slightly increased to ₹46,269 crore. The company's board recommended a dividend of ₹7.30 per equity share for the financial year 2023-24.

articles.headline: US springs forced-labour tariffs on India, 59 others
articles.summary: The US proposed tariffs on India and 59 other countries citing failure to prohibit imports of goods made with forced labour. India is engaged with the US on the proposed 12.5% levy, with a trade deal possibly in weeks.

articles.headline: True North to sell Cloudnine stake as co eyes fresh funds
articles.summary: Private equity firm True North plans to sell its stake in Cloudnine Hospitals as the company seeks $250-300 million in fresh funding. Proceeds will be used for inorganic plans in the Middle East.

articles.headline: PMI relief for India
articles.summary: India's manufacturing PMI climbed to a three-month high of 55.0 in May, while services PMI reached a six-month high of 59.8, signaling strong economic performance driven by domestic factors.

articles.headline: Obesity rises among adult Indians
articles.summary: National Family Health Survey shows 30.7% of women and 27.3% of men are overweight or obese. Lifestyle changes and processed food consumption are key reasons, linked to higher diabetes and cardiac risks.

articles.headline: New economics of Indian weddings
articles.summary: A Mint survey reveals young Indians still spend big on weddings but prefer intimate destination celebrations. High earners borrow more, while gold and silver are losing appeal.

articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi expressed confidence that India can achieve a $5 trillion economy, emphasizing reforms and a favourable global environment. He highlighted the country's potential during the India-US Strategic Partnership Forum event.

articles.summary: Wipro is reducing employee pay, while indices have seen an increase. This juxtaposition highlights contrasting trends in the corporate and stock markets.

articles.headline: UPI links up with merchants for payments
articles.summary: Unified Payments Interface (UPI) is now linked with merchants for seamless transactions. This integration aims to simplify digital payments for consumers and businesses.

articles.headline: Start-up companies race to build physical data centres
articles.summary: Start-up companies are accelerating efforts to build physical data centres. This surge in infrastructure development is driven by increasing demand for data storage and processing.

articles.headline: India strong interest despite govt policy
articles.summary: India continues to attract strong investment interest, despite current government policies. This resilience indicates underlying economic potential and market appeal.

articles.headline: Emids bets big on India GCC playbook
articles.summary: Emids is significantly investing in India's Global Capability Centres (GCCs). This expansion highlights the country's growing role in global business services.

articles.headline: First flight to India in 17 yrs back to flight hrs from regular flights
articles.summary: A flight to India, after 17 years, has resumed regular flight hours. This marks a significant development in air travel connectivity.

articles.headline: She left Sikh-ism, thinking NEFT was over
articles.summary: A person left Sikhism, believing the NEFT process was concluded. This unusual decision raises questions about personal beliefs and financial procedures.

articles.headline: NEET-like data shows rise in students
articles.summary: Data similar to NEET examinations indicates a rise in student enrollment. This trend suggests increased competition and demand for higher education.

articles.headline: Psychological woes
articles.summary: A report on psychological issues highlights growing concerns among individuals. The article delves into the prevalence and impact of mental health challenges.

articles.headline: He knew everything: why Messi's childhood coach threw in the towel
articles.summary: Messi's childhood coach reveals how the football legend's early talent was overwhelming. The coach resigned due to Messi's exceptional skills, making coaching him impossible.

articles.headline: Maja, diamond in the rough
articles.summary: Polish Girl continues Dream Run, Shnaider Stuns Sabalenka. The article discusses the performance of young tennis players in a major tournament.

articles.headline: Sherpa wins Virat's Trophy
articles.summary: Sherpa has emerged victorious, winning the Virat's Trophy. This sporting achievement marks a significant milestone for the athlete.

articles.headline: Surya could lose job
articles.summary: Surya might lose his job, according to reports. The article speculates on potential career changes for the individual.

articles.headline: It's Pragg-magic as Carlsen blunders again
articles.summary: Pragg's exceptional play has led to a blunder by Carlsen. This chess match highlights the rising talent of young players in the sport.

articles.headline: India's women top table
articles.summary: The Indian women's team has achieved the top position in a recent ranking. This success reflects their strong performance and dedication.

articles.headline: Canada beat hosts
articles.summary: Canada has defeated the host nation in a sporting event. This victory marks a significant achievement for the Canadian team.

articles.headline: Russian forces block heated doors
articles.summary: Russian forces have reportedly blocked heated doors, indicating potential military actions or strategic maneuvers. The situation remains tense.

articles.headline: How Cubans keep going, despite American pressure and fuel blockade
articles.summary: The article explores how Cubans are persevering despite US pressure and a fuel blockade. It highlights the resilience of the nation and its people.

articles.headline: More timber, more global blunder
articles.summary: The article discusses the implications of increased timber trade and potential global blunders. It raises environmental and economic concerns.

articles.headline: The World Cup: A superstitions celebration
articles.summary: The article delves into the superstitions surrounding the World Cup. It highlights how beliefs and rituals play a role in the global sporting event.

articles.headline: England target for Ballon d'Or
articles.summary: England's player is a target for the Ballon d'Or award. The article discusses his potential and the recognition he might receive.

articles.headline: Safety gaps spark another Delhi tragedy as 21 die in B&B blaze
articles.summary: An early morning fire in Flourish Stay, a Hauz Rani bed-and-breakfast, killed at least 21 people, including 12 foreign nationals. Significant violations included 26 rooms instead of six allowed, no fire exit, and an illegal restaurant. Owner Lavkesh Bajaj was arrested for culpable homicide. 26 injured. The crowded area hindered rescue.

articles.headline: TMC rift widens; Ritabrata states he has LoP status
articles.summary: Fifty-eight Trinamool Congress lawmakers disowned party general secretary Abhishek Banerjee and pleaded with Mamata Banerjee to be their adviser. Expelled legislator Ritabrata Banerjee announced his appointment as leader of the Opposition, effectively splitting the party and jeopardizing its symbol and name. The party dissolved all organizational committees.

articles.headline: ED summons TMC MP Abhishek Banerjee in PMLA probe
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate summoned Trinamool Congress (TMC) national general secretary and MP Abhishek Banerjee to appear on June 15 for a money laundering probe in the primary teachers' recruitment scam in West Bengal. Summons delivered at his Kalighat residence.

articles.headline: Cabinet clears ₹9.5k-cr scheme to replace BS-IV vehicles in NCR
articles.summary: The Union government approved a ₹9,585-crore scheme to replace about 200,000 BS-IV heavy vehicles (191,000 trucks, 16,329 buses) in Delhi-NCR with BS-VI, CNG, or electric modes to curb air pollution. Owners get incentives like 5% interest subvention and discounts for scrapping older vehicles.

articles.headline: Jaishankar to meet British FM to review bilateral ties
articles.summary: External affairs minister S Jaishankar will meet UK foreign minister Yvette Cooper to review bilateral relations and discuss minimising economic shocks from ongoing conflicts. Cooper's visit follows UK business secretary Peter Kyle's trip, aimed at pushing the Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA).

articles.headline: SpaceX IPO to put Elon Musk just shy of $1 trillion
articles.summary: SpaceX is planning to offer shares in an upcoming IPO at $135 each, which would place Elon Musk’s net worth at $988 billion, according to Bloomberg Billionaires Index. A slight increase to $138 per share could make him a trillionaire when trading begins around June 12.

articles.headline: Rubio: Quad leaders’ meet likely this year, on APEC sidelines
articles.summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said efforts are underway for a Quad leaders meeting this year, potentially on the sidelines of another global gathering, such as the Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation Economic Leaders’ Meeting in Shenzhen, China, in November.

articles.headline: Dodgy licences among raft of violations at Delhi B&B fire
articles.summary: The Hauz Rani fire revealed a web of violations: a 26-room hotel operating under a 6-room B&B license, a full-fledged restaurant disguised as a snack outlet, and no sanctioned building plan or fire safety NOC. These lapses contributed to 21 deaths in Delhi's deadliest fire in years.

articles.headline: Locals saved lives with mattresses, bedsheets at Delhi B&B fire
articles.summary: Neighbors and shopkeepers were first responders at the Hauz Rani B&B fire, improvising rescues with mattresses and bedsheets for people jumping from windows. Thick smoke filled staircases, trapping guests. Volunteers aided firefighters, some administering CPR, as the inferno killed 21.

articles.headline: Couple found dead locked in embrace in Delhi B&B fire
articles.summary: Firefighters found a couple dead in a locked washroom on the first floor of the burning Hauz Rani B&B, appearing to hold each other. The basement entrance was blocked, hindering rescues. The fire killed 21, with many trapped due to smoke and lack of clear escape routes.

articles.headline: Karnataka CM Shivakumar announces free bus passes for students
articles.summary: Karnataka CM DK Shivakumar announced free bus passes for all school and college students, regardless of financial implications, as his cabinet's first major decision. This expands a previous scheme for women, aiming to boost youth development and address employment.

articles.headline: BK Hariprasad replaces DKS as new KPCC chief
articles.summary: The AICC appointed veteran leader BK Hariprasad as the new Karnataka Pradesh Congress Committee (KPCC) chief, replacing DK Shivakumar. Hariprasad was also nominated for the Karnataka Legislative Council elections. This comes as Shivakumar assumes the chief minister's office.

articles.headline: Congress names former Karnataka CM Siddaramaiah as CWC member
articles.summary: The AICC appointed Karnataka's outgoing chief minister Siddharamaiah as a member of the Congress Working Committee (CWC). This decision was confirmed ahead of DK Shivakumar's swearing-in as the new CM, following discussions with party high command in Delhi.

articles.headline: New Karnataka ministers pledge commitment to state development
articles.summary: Following their induction into the Karnataka cabinet, new ministers pledged commitment to state development under CM DK Shivakumar. Sharan Prakash Patil emphasized focus on development-oriented governance, while Ramalinga Reddy promised to complete all election promises within two years.

articles.headline: Karnataka DyCM says experience not overlooked after missing top post
articles.summary: Karnataka Deputy CM G Parameshwara denied his experience was overlooked, stating he accepted the party's decision and the important responsibility. He expressed gratitude for the trust, committed to serving the state according to Congress ideology and manifesto.

articles.headline: Opposition congratulates Karnataka CM, criticises Congress performance
articles.summary: Opposition parties congratulated Karnataka CM DK Shivakumar but criticised Congress, alleging "non-performance" led to the leadership change. R Ashoka (LoP) said the new CM faces debris of failures. BJP MP Ashok Bajpai claimed factionalism would make public welfare impossible.

articles.headline: Caste factors influenced Karnataka CM selection, says Congress MLA
articles.summary: Congress MLA Ashok Pattan expressed disappointment about not being included in the new Karnataka cabinet, alleging caste considerations played a role in the chief minister's selection. He hopes for his name to be in the second list of ministers.

articles.headline: TTD ghee fraud case: ED seizes ₹60 lakh cash
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) seized ₹60 lakh cash and identified properties worth ₹45 crore during searches at 15 locations in Maharashtra, Uttarakhand, Delhi, and Andhra Pradesh, in a money-laundering probe related to alleged ghee adulteration fraud linked to the Tirumala Tirupati Devasthanams (TTD) trust.

articles.headline: Girl appears major: Kerala HC grants bail to husband in kidnapping case
articles.summary: The Kerala High Court granted transit bail to Mohd Farman, accused of kidnapping his wife, stating that her birth certificate prima facie shows her as a major (born January 1, 2008). Madhya Pradesh police registered the case based on her father's complaint alleging she was a minor.

articles.headline: PMK leader Ramadoss seeks full farm loan waiver in Tamil Nadu
articles.summary: PMK leader Anbumani Ramadoss urged the Tamil Nadu government to implement a complete waiver of farmers' loans, criticising current relief measures as inadequate compared to states like Maharashtra, which waived ₹36,585 crore for 5.5 million farmers.

articles.headline: Mother’s claim to child not doubted: Madras HC on kidney transplant
articles.summary: The Madras High Court ruled that a mother's claim to her child should not be casually doubted, setting aside an order denying a Bangladeshi woman permission to donate a kidney to her minor son due to unestablished marital status. The court emphasized the sacred mother-child relationship.

articles.headline: Mahesh Kumar Aggarwal takes charge as Tamil Nadu DGP
articles.summary: Senior IPS officer Mahesh Kumar Aggarwal assumed office as Tamil Nadu Director General of Police (DGP), succeeding Sandeep Rai Rathore. He assured stringent measures against drug abuse, crimes against children, and cybercrime, emphasizing maintaining law and order.

articles.headline: KTR to take out statewide padayatra in 2027: BRS
articles.summary: Bharat Rashtra Samithi (BRS) working president K T Rama Rao announced a statewide padayatra in Telangana in 2027 to reconnect with people and expose failures of the Congress government. He ruled out alliances with BJP and predicted a BRS return to power with 90 seats.

articles.headline: Leaders pay tribute to former Tamil Nadu CM Karunanidhi
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu minister A Rajmohan, DMK president M K Stalin, and other leaders paid rich tributes to former CM M Karunanidhi on his 103rd birth anniversary. Stalin posted on social media about Karunanidhi's tireless work for Tamil people, while Congress praised his commitment to equality.

articles.headline: Kerala LDF walks out over officials’ mass transfer row
articles.summary: The Opposition Left Democratic Front (LDF) staged a walkout from the Kerala Assembly after its adjournment motion on alleged mass transfer of government officials by the newly elected UDF government was disallowed. Leader Pinarayi Vijayan alleged nearly 2,000 unfair transfers violating rules.

articles.headline: Andhra, Telangana parties brace for voter list scrutiny
articles.summary: Major political parties in Andhra Pradesh and Telangana are preparing for the special intensive revision (SIR) of electoral rolls. YSRCP and TDP are taking precautions against manipulation, while Telangana Congress raised concerns about rushing the process and sought deferment.

articles.headline: AP farmers oppose land acquisition for Amaravati capital project
articles.summary: Farmers in Undavalli and Penumaka villages are resisting land acquisition for Andhra Pradesh’s Amaravati capital city project, boycotting a gram sabha. They contend their lands are highly fertile and along a national highway, making their value higher than compensated by land pooling schemes.

articles.headline: Mamata allowed Kolkata mayor to resign, says Ghosh
articles.summary: Senior Trinamool Congress leader Firhad Hakim is set to resign as Kolkata mayor after Mamata Banerjee accepted his request. Party spokesperson Kunal Ghosh stated Hakim had repeatedly expressed this desire. This comes amid a serious internal rebellion in TMC, with many legislators backing Ritabrata Banerjee.

articles.headline: Karnataka cabinet features mix of castes, minority faces
articles.summary: The new Karnataka cabinet led by DK Shivakumar reflects social engineering, with two Vokkaliga, three Lingayat, three Dalit, two Kuruba leaders, and one Muslim and one Christian minister. Nine of 14 ministers are from southern Karnataka, five from northern. No women are included.

articles.headline: NEET paper leak, CBSE OSM to be key focus of INDIA bloc meet
articles.summary: The INDIA bloc will focus on the NEET exam paper leak and on-screen marking (OSM) controversy in CBSE Class XII answersheets at its upcoming meeting. This comes after the BJP won 207 seats to TMC's 80, and Congress broke ties with DMK.

articles.headline: SC: Courts must end ‘stale, frozen’ marriages
articles.summary: The Supreme Court urged courts to close prolonged marital disputes by granting divorce in "stale and frozen" relationships, even if statutory grounds aren't strictly met. This approach, using Article 142 powers, serves both spouses and society by allowing individuals to rebuild lives.

articles.headline: Father held for murder of UP man found in car
articles.summary: Police arrested Sundar Tyagi and an associate for killing Tyagi's 25-year-old son, Tushar, over a land dispute in Hapur district, Uttar Pradesh. Tushar was found shot dead in his SUV. He wanted to sell 40 bighas of agricultural land, which his father opposed.

articles.headline: Delhi HC allows embryo transfer beyond legal age limit
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court allowed a woman to undergo frozen embryo transfer despite exceeding the 50-year age limit prescribed by the ART Act, stating reproductive rights cannot be defeated by technical readings of law. The couple had started IVF treatment before the woman crossed the limit.

articles.headline: India ahead of US in 2025 solar capacity additions
articles.summary: India became the world’s second-largest solar growth market in 2025, surpassing the USA in annual solar capacity additions, according to Renewable Energy Minister Pralhad Joshi. India recorded over 37 GW additions, compared to USA’s 34 GW, driven by strong policy support and infrastructure.

articles.headline: MHA updates visa manual, ends dual approval process
articles.summary: The Centre removed the requirement of separate Prior Reference Category (PRC) clearance for foreign nationals already granted security clearance via the Security Clearance Portal (SCP). This streamlines approvals for categories like people from Afghanistan, Pakistan, Iraq, Sudan, China, and those of Pakistani origin.

articles.headline: India-Bangladesh border forces to hold talks in Delhi from June 8
articles.summary: The 57th Director General-level Border Coordination Conference between India’s BSF and Bangladesh’s BGB will be held in New Delhi from June 8-11. Talks will focus on preventing assaults, curbing trans-border crimes, preventing entry of Bangladeshi criminals, and addressing Indian Insurgent Groups (IIGs) in Bangladesh.

articles.headline: Union ministers carpool to cabinet meeting, PM Modi urged fuel saving
articles.summary: Several Union ministers carpooled to Wednesday’s cabinet meeting after PM Narendra Modi urged them to save fuel. Nitin Gadkari, JP Nadda, Kiren Rijiju, and Pralhad Joshi were among those who shared cars.

articles.headline: Gadkari to attend Zojila tunnel event on June 9
articles.summary: Union minister Nitin Gadkari will attend the breakthrough ceremony at the strategically important Zojila tunnel, located at the border of J&K and Ladakh, on June 9.

articles.headline: Kewal Singh Dhillon takes charge as BJP Punjab president
articles.summary: The Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) appointed 76-year-old industrialist-turned-politician Kewal Singh Dhillon as its first-ever Jat Sikh state president in Punjab. Haryana CM Nayab Singh Saini and outgoing state chief Sunil Jakhar were in attendance.

articles.headline: Court frames charges against Jacqueline, Sukesh in PMLA case
articles.summary: A Delhi court formally framed charges against actor Jacqueline Fernandez, alleged conman Sukesh Chandrasekhar, and 16 others in a ₹200-crore money laundering case. Chandrasekhar also faces charges under MCOCA in a separate extortion case.

articles.headline: Woman alleges confinement, torture by husband, in-laws in Dehradun
articles.summary: Police in Dehradun are investigating allegations from a woman that her husband and in-laws confined her in a toilet for 10 months, repeatedly assaulted her, and separated her from her twins. An FIR has been registered.

articles.headline: 5 held for raping 15-year-old for months in Assam
articles.summary: Five men were arrested in Assam’s Biswanath district for allegedly gang-raping a 15-year-old girl multiple times over several months. The case came to light when locals spotted a vehicle and rescued the victim; she later identified the accused.

articles.headline: Man blames NEET leak for daughter's suicide in Maharashtra
articles.summary: Family members of a 20-year-old NEET aspirant who died by suicide in Nagpur claim the paper leak and exam cancellation drove her to the extreme step. They found a suicide note where she expressed shattered confidence after the news broke.

articles.headline: CBSE thwarts fresh cyber attack, receives 56,000 re-evaluation applications
articles.summary: CBSE successfully thwarted a 3.8 million-packet denial-of-service (DoS) attack on its website. Over 56,000 applications for verification and re-evaluation were received, with payment gateways from several banks available for processing.

articles.headline: Family in hiding as Abhijeet Dipke’s political steps spark worries
articles.summary: The family of Boston-based Abhijeet Dipke is in hiding, concerned for their safety as the nation awaits his return for a protest. Dipke, founder of the Cockroach Janata Party (CJP), plans a peaceful protest from Delhi airport to Jantar Mantar to address education system failures.

articles.headline: Cockroach Janata Party open to talks with government and opposition
articles.summary: The Cockroach Janata Party (CJP), demanding the education minister's resignation over examination irregularities, is open to dialogue with both government and opposition. CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke named three spokespersons to represent the collective, ahead of a planned protest in Delhi.

articles.headline: 200,000 opt to pay $100K for faster H1B visa processing
articles.summary: US Homeland Security secretary Markwayne Mullin stated 200,000 applicants paid the $100,000 H-1B visa application fee for faster, 15-day processing. The remaining 80,000 applicants waited 7.5 months. Senator Collins highlighted difficulties for rural hospitals in hiring surgeons due to the fee.

articles.headline: Several fall ill due to water contamination in Ahmedabad
articles.summary: Around 50 people were reported ill with diarrhoea and nausea in Ahmedabad's Ghatlodia area over four days due to contaminated Narmada water supply. A leaking pipeline caused sewage to mix with drinking water. Residents had flagged the problem since Friday.

articles.headline: SC collegium recommends appointment of High Court judges
articles.summary: The Supreme Court Collegium recommended the appointment of seven advocates as judges for the high courts of Karnataka and Madhya Pradesh. It also recommended elevating two additional judges as permanent judges for the Punjab and Haryana High Court.

articles.headline: Trump admits calling Netanyahu ‘crazy’ in phone call
articles.summary: Donald Trump acknowledged criticising Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu as "crazy" during a phone call, expressing "perturbation" that Israel's fight with Hezbollah hindered Iran peace talks. Despite this, Trump insisted their relationship was solid and that both are "wartime" leaders.

articles.headline: Less immigration led to economic weakness: Canadian PM Carney
articles.summary: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney attributed the country's technical recession, with the economy contracting 0.1% in the first quarter, partly to his government’s decision to reduce immigration.

articles.headline: New Solomon Islands PM to review China security pact
articles.summary: New Solomon Islands Prime Minister Matthew Wale announced he would review a secretive 2022 security pact with Beijing. Australia and the United States have been critical of the deal, fearing it could allow a permanent Chinese navy presence in the South Pacific.

articles.headline: Indian-Americans face tough night in US primary elections
articles.summary: Indian-American politicians faced challenges in US primary elections. Nithya Raman trailed in the LA mayoral primary. Saikat Chakrabarti was edged out in California’s 14th congressional district. Rakhi Israni lost, and Tina Shah finished second in New Jersey. Incumbents Ami Bera and Ro Khanna topped their primaries.

articles.headline: Nine dead as Israel strikes Beirut, south Lebanon
articles.summary: Lebanon reported Israeli strikes killed at least nine people in its south, including two paramedics, with another raid hitting a car near Beirut. The Israeli military said it intercepted "hostile aircraft" and projectiles from Lebanon.

articles.headline: British Sikh man’s trial for murder sparks anti-police protests in UK
articles.summary: A British Sikh man's murder conviction led to violent anti-police protests in Southampton, UK, with officers targeted over racism allegations. Protesters heard from activists condemning "two-tier policing." Prime Minister Keir Starmer called for an end to violence and disorder.

articles.headline: UAE seeks united Gulf stance after attacks by Iran
articles.summary: A top Emirati official called for a united Gulf stance after renewed Iranian attacks on Kuwait and Bahrain. UAE presidential advisor Anwar Gargash emphasized that the security of GCC states is interconnected.

articles.headline: First oil supertanker at Iran’s Kharg Island in a month
articles.summary: The first oil supertanker in almost a month was spotted moored at Iran’s Kharg Island, according to an image taken by the EU’s Sentinel 1 satellite. No similarly-sized ships had been seen at the island since May 6.

articles.headline: Wind and Shnaider sweep aside Sabalenka in French Open quarters
articles.summary: Unseeded Maja Chwalinska and 25th seed Diana Shnaider caused major upsets in the French Open women's singles quarter-finals. Chwalinska beat Anna Kalinskaya, and Shnaider came from behind to defeat world No.1 Aryna Sabalenka 3-6, 7-5, 6-0, ensuring a first-time Grand Slam champion.

articles.headline: Dipanshi Phogat hopes to shine on Asian Games debut
articles.summary: 17-year-old wrestler Dipanshi Phogat, from Haryana's Rohtak district, sealed her Asian Games debut in the 50kg bracket at the trials. Despite the chaos surrounding other wrestlers, she maintained focus and aims for gold, marking a significant step in her promising career.

articles.headline: Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty pull out of Indonesia Open
articles.summary: Indian badminton duo Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty pulled out of the Indonesia Open during their opening round match, trailing 6-11, due to Satwik pointing to his right shoulder. They had won the Singapore Open last week.

articles.headline: India set for full New Zealand cricket tour
articles.summary: India will play 12 international matches in New Zealand later this year, including five T20Is, five ODIs, and two Tests. The all-format tour, billed as the biggest in a generation for New Zealand, starts October 22 in Christchurch.

articles.headline: Colombia’s jersey embroiled in presidential election politics
articles.summary: Colombia’s national football jersey has become embroiled in the country's presidential election, with supporters of Abelardo de la Espriella wearing it at political rallies. Sen. Iván Cepeda criticized this as an "opportunistic act" of stealing a national symbol, sparking debate on its political use.

articles.headline: Iran, G-2 Trade Strikes in Persian Gulf Further Testing Ceasefire
articles.summary: The US imposed sanctions on Iran targeting its energy and petrochemical sectors, while also imposing new sanctions on entities involved in missile programs. Simultaneously, the US announced new trade measures against China, further escalating trade tensions.

articles.headline: Tech Stocks Fall Sharply on Reaching Markets, Beating Volatility
articles.summary: Technology stocks experienced a significant downturn, with major indices seeing substantial drops. This decline is attributed to a combination of factors, including investor concerns about valuations and broader market volatility. Several key tech companies saw their share prices decrease.

articles.headline: Monsoon Likely to Make Landfall in Kerala Today
articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has predicted that the Southwest Monsoon is likely to make landfall in Kerala today. The monsoon season is crucial for India's agricultural sector and overall economy, with expectations of normal rainfall this year.

articles.headline: India Gets a Foot in Project Glassing Door
articles.summary: India has secured a position in the top 10 countries for attracting foreign direct investment (FDI) in the construction sector. The country's growing infrastructure development and favorable investment policies have contributed to this achievement.

articles.headline: New Delhi: India Sets up an Average Real Estate Company
articles.summary: India has established an average real estate company, with a focus on providing affordable housing solutions. The company aims to cater to the growing demand for housing in urban areas and contribute to the country's infrastructure development.

articles.headline: Rahul's People's Trust to Congress Our Greatest Responsibility
articles.summary: Congress leader Rahul Gandhi emphasized that upholding the trust of the people is the Congress party's highest responsibility. He highlighted the party's commitment to serving the masses and addressing their concerns with dedication and integrity.

articles.headline: Karnataka's Bandé to 'K'tanaka' Leaders
articles.summary: A political realignment is underway in Karnataka, with leaders from various factions converging to form a new coalition. This development is expected to shape the state's political landscape and impact future governance.

articles.headline: Hero MotoCorp CEO Pawan Munjal Joins as Advisor
articles.summary: Pawan Munjal, the CEO of Hero MotoCorp, has been appointed as an advisor to the company's board. His expertise and leadership are expected to further strengthen the company's strategic direction and growth initiatives.

articles.headline: India Plans Additional 12.5% Duty on Imports
articles.summary: India is planning to impose an additional 12.5% duty on select imported goods. This move is aimed at boosting domestic manufacturing and reducing reliance on foreign products, aligning with the 'Make in India' initiative.

articles.headline: Claude, Other AI Tools Used to Breach CBSE Panels: IIT Panel
articles.summary: An IIT panel has revealed that AI tools, including Claude and other advanced technologies, were used to breach the Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) examination panels. This raises serious concerns about the security of digital systems.

articles.headline: As Mamata Struggles, 'Secular Rivals' Eye Opposition Space
articles.summary: Mamata Banerjee faces internal challenges within her party as 'secular rivals' eye the opposition space. This political maneuver aims to consolidate influence and challenge her leadership in the upcoming elections.

articles.headline: Not a Minor Issue! Andhra Pradesh CM Revises Major Matter with Centre
articles.summary: Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister Y.S. Jagan Mohan Reddy has reportedly raised a significant issue with the central government, seeking revisions on a major matter. The specifics of the issue remain undisclosed but are expected to impact state-federal relations.

articles.headline: This Red Card Shakes Up Mizoram's Prohibition Policy
articles.summary: A controversial 'red card' system introduced in Mizoram for liquor consumption and possession has caused a stir. The policy aims to curb alcohol abuse but has faced criticism for its strictness and potential implications.

articles.headline: US Economy: Jobs Growth to 4.3% in Q1 2023
articles.summary: The US economy witnessed job growth of 4.3% in the first quarter of 2023. This positive trend indicates a resilient labor market and contributes to overall economic stability, despite global uncertainties.

articles.headline: Environmental Friendly Solutions for Energy Sector
articles.summary: Gujarat is leading the way in adopting environmentally friendly solutions for its energy sector. The state is championing renewable energy sources and sustainable practices to meet its growing energy demands.

articles.headline: Engineering a Sustainable Future
articles.summary: The article discusses the crucial role of industrial output and chemical engineering in achieving a sustainable future. It highlights the need for innovation in resource utilization and waste management to address environmental challenges.

articles.headline: Industrial Shed Available for Lease
articles.summary: A large industrial shed measuring 2,400 sq ft is available for lease in a prime location. The property is suitable for various industrial activities and offers excellent connectivity to major transportation networks.

articles.headline: Home Cooked Chili on: Costlier Inputs May Rise
articles.summary: The cost of home-cooked meals is expected to rise due to increasing input prices for essential ingredients. This trend could impact household budgets and consumer spending patterns, especially for staple food items.

articles.headline: Aurobindo Pharma Forays into Biologics Contract Production
articles.summary: Aurobindo Pharma has entered the biologics contract production space, investing $1,200 crore to establish a facility. This strategic move aims to expand its offerings and tap into the growing biologics market.

articles.headline: Drug Exports Likely to Stay Subdued on Policy Risks
articles.summary: India's drug exports are projected to remain subdued due to policy uncertainties and regulatory challenges. The industry is seeking clarity and stability in government policies to boost international trade and competitiveness.

articles.headline: Wind in its Sails, Suzlon Targets 10-GW Annual Release
articles.summary: Suzlon Energy aims to achieve a 10-GW annual release of wind power projects, capitalizing on the growing demand for renewable energy. The company is focused on expanding its capacity and contributing to India's clean energy goals.

articles.headline: Build Wealth Beyond Your Salary
articles.summary: A masterclass on building wealth beyond salary is being offered. The two-day live online event features expert speakers like Sanjiv Kathuria, a CFA and MBA finance, sharing insights on financial strategies.

articles.headline: Ola Parent Sees Peak Valuation Down 99% to $70m
articles.summary: Ola, the ride-hailing giant, has seen its valuation plummet by 99%, falling from $7.3 billion to $70 million. This significant drop reflects market challenges and investor sentiment towards the company.

articles.headline: AI Prescription: Infy Bags 7-Yr Deal to Upgrade Fortis-owner IHHI's Network
articles.summary: Infosys (Infy) has secured a 7-year contract with IHHI, the owner of Fortis Healthcare, to upgrade its network infrastructure. This deal highlights Infy's expanding presence in the healthcare IT sector.

articles.headline: Premium Grocery Startup FirstClub Raises $5m
articles.summary: FirstClub, a premium grocery startup, has successfully raised $5 million in funding. This investment is expected to fuel its expansion and enhance its offerings in the competitive e-commerce market.

articles.headline: Pearson to Scale Up Presence
articles.summary: Pearson is planning to scale up its operations in India, focusing on its educational services and digital learning platforms. The company aims to cater to the growing demand for quality education and skill development.

articles.headline: Peak XV Talks to Lead $10m Round in Voice Firm Ringg
articles.summary: Peak XV Partners is reportedly in talks to lead a $10 million funding round for Ringg, a voice-focused technology firm. This investment could accelerate Ringg's growth and expansion in the communication sector.

articles.summary: Mastercard plans to focus on its long-term business by targeting tier 3 and 4 markets for growth. The company aims to expand its reach and services in these emerging regions, leveraging digital payment solutions.

articles.summary: WPP, a global advertising giant, forecasts that the advertising market will grow by 2026, despite current volatility. This projection indicates optimism for the sector's recovery and expansion.

articles.summary: ADI and Korea's NPS are reportedly involved in a development concerning Carlsberg, ahead of a planned market debut. This suggests a significant corporate event or transaction in the beverage industry.

articles.summary: Six French hotels are highlighted for their luxury brand prestige. The article likely details their offerings, history, and contributions to the luxury hospitality sector.

articles.headline: Luxury brands seek to lure AI super-rich
articles.summary: Luxury brands are targeting the 'AI super-rich' by tailoring their products and services to this affluent demographic. The article explores how fashion houses are adapting to the evolving preferences of wealthy consumers.

articles.headline: Artist sues over Dallas destruction of Wall mural
articles.summary: An artist has filed a lawsuit following the destruction of a mural in Dallas. The legal action stems from alleged unauthorized removal or damage to the artwork, raising questions about artistic rights.

articles.headline: Polish Qualifier Resumes
articles.summary: A Polish qualifier has resumed their competition after a break. The article likely provides updates on their progress and performance in the ongoing event.

articles.headline: New Stadium, Same World: Why Stadium Venues are Getting Rebranded
articles.summary: The article explores the trend of rebranding stadium venues, even when the stadiums themselves are new. It examines the strategic reasons behind these naming rights deals and their impact on fan engagement.

articles.headline: State banks urged to build buffers against rising loan stress
articles.summary: The finance ministry has directed state-owned banks to increase provisioning buffers for retail, MSME, and agriculture loans. This proactive measure aims to strengthen banks' financial resilience ahead of the anticipated rollout of RBI's expected credit loss (ECL) regime.

articles.headline: SEBI flags ₹15 trillion revenue misrepresentation by Rajesh Exports
articles.summary: SEBI alleges Rajesh Exports misrepresented nearly all its revenue (₹15.15 lakh crore or 99.8%) from subsidiaries over five years (FY21-FY25). The probe, triggered by a shareholder complaint, cited inconsistencies and non-disclosure of consolidated operations, particularly involving its Swiss subsidiary Valcambi SA.

articles.headline: Indian killed in Iran's drone strike on Kuwait airport
articles.summary: An Iranian drone attack on Kuwait's main airport killed one Indian national and injured 63 others, escalating regional tensions. Kuwait briefly closed its airport, and India condemned the targeting of civilian infrastructure. Kuwait is expelling two Iranian diplomats.

articles.headline: Tata Motors to license Chery EV platform for premium electric vehicles
articles.summary: Tata Motors plans to license an EV platform from China's Chery to develop its delayed premium electric vehicles, with the first model slated for 2027. This strategic move aims to maintain Tata's leading position in India's EV market.

articles.headline: Apple agrees to submit India financials in antitrust probe
articles.summary: Apple has agreed to provide its India business financials to the Competition Commission of India (CCI) for an antitrust investigation. The CCI previously found the company abused its market position in the iPhone apps market.

articles.headline: NHPC OFS fully subscribed, government garners ₹4,300 crore
articles.summary: The Offer for Sale (OFS) in NHPC Ltd saw full subscription, enabling the government to raise approximately ₹4,300 crore through its stake dilution.

articles.headline: AI bubble burst could benefit India, fostering leadership and reducing tech reliance
articles.summary: A potential AI bubble burst globally could strategically benefit India. It might slow down international AI capital expenditure, creating an opportunity for India to emerge as a leader and decrease its dependence on foreign AI technology.

articles.headline: Cabinet approves ₹10,000 crore ATF price stabilization fund
articles.summary: The Union Cabinet approved a ₹10,000 crore one-time budgetary support for a price stabilization fund to cushion airlines from escalating jet fuel prices, with an additional ₹9,585 crore for a clean transport scheme in Delhi-NCR.

articles.headline: Delhi hotel fire kills 21, including foreigners
articles.summary: A fire at a six-storey hotel in South Delhi's Malviya Nagar killed 21 people, including 12 foreign nationals, and injured 47. The facility operated without fire clearance, and its owner was apprehended.

articles.headline: US proposes 12.5% tariff on Indian goods over forced labor concerns
articles.summary: The U.S. proposed a 12.5% tariff on imports from India and 53 other nations, citing alleged failure to enforce prohibitions on goods made with forced labor. This action stems from a Section 301 investigation under the U.S. Trade Act.

articles.headline: Centre approves ₹9,585 crore scheme to cut Delhi-NCR pollution
articles.summary: The Union Cabinet approved a ₹9,585 crore scheme to reduce air pollution in Delhi-NCR. It incentivizes owners of trucks and buses to switch to BS-VI or electric vehicles over two years.

articles.headline: D.K. Shivakumar sworn in as Karnataka Chief Minister
articles.summary: D.K. Shivakumar took oath as the 25th Chief Minister of Karnataka, accompanied by 13 Cabinet Ministers, including Deputy CM G. Parameshwara. He announced free bus passes for students and the formation of 10,000 Bharat Jodo Yuva Sanghas.

articles.headline: Expelled Trinamool MLA appointed Bengal Assembly LoP
articles.summary: Expelled Trinamool Congress MLA Ritabrata Banerjee has been appointed Leader of the Opposition in the West Bengal Assembly with the backing of 58 MLAs. Kolkata Mayor Firhad Hakim resigned from his post.

articles.headline: Over 40,000 Class 12 students seek CBSE answer sheet re-evaluation
articles.summary: More than 40,000 Class 12 students have applied to the CBSE for verification and re-evaluation of their answer sheets by the June 6 deadline. The process has been affected by technical glitches.

articles.headline: Systemic gaps in fire safety enforcement highlighted
articles.summary: Former Delhi Fire Services Director G.C. Mishra pointed out systemic failures in fire safety enforcement and building code compliance, citing unauthorized constructions and inadequate safety infrastructure as significant issues.

articles.headline: ‘No PUC, No Fuel’ rule in 8 UP NCR districts from October 1
articles.summary: Starting October 1, vehicles without valid Pollution Under Control (PUC) certificates will be denied fuel in eight Uttar Pradesh districts within the NCR. Automatic number plate recognition cameras will enforce this rule.

articles.headline: Kerala seeks Centre's help for NEET and CBSE exam evaluation issues
articles.summary: The Kerala government has requested the Union Education Minister's intervention to address suspected evaluation glitches and crises in NEET and CBSE examinations.

articles.headline: TVK allots lone Rajya Sabha seat from Tamil Nadu to Congress
articles.summary: The Tamil Maanila Congress (TVK) surprised many by allocating its sole Rajya Sabha seat from Tamil Nadu to the Congress, increasing the Congress's representation from the state to three.

articles.headline: Congress criticizes Maharashtra farm loan waiver for excluding farmers
articles.summary: Maharashtra Congress president Harshvardhan Sapkal claimed that over 50% of farmers will be excluded from the state's farm loan waiver scheme due to strict eligibility criteria, labeling it a 'complete sham'.

articles.headline: Punjab launches Montessori model in Anganwadis
articles.summary: Punjab has initiated its first Montessori education program in government anganwadis and pre-primary schools in Hoshiarpur district, aiming to promote independence, creativity, and hands-on learning.

articles.headline: K. Annamalai quits BJP, to launch new political movement
articles.summary: Former Tamil Nadu BJP president K. Annamalai has resigned from the party and is expected to launch a new regional political movement.

articles.headline: SIT claims evidence of shielding officers in security detail case
articles.summary: The Special Investigation Team (SIT) probing former Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan's security detail stated it found evidence suggesting an attempt to protect accused officers from prosecution by falsifying records.

articles.headline: Punjab BJP chief: AAP's days in power are numbered
articles.summary: Punjab BJP president Kewal Singh Dhillon criticized the AAP-led state government, predicting its downfall in the 2027 Assembly elections and asserting that the BJP would oust them.

articles.headline: Telangana ACB arrests suspended police officer in disproportionate assets case
articles.summary: The Telangana Anti-Corruption Bureau (ACB) arrested suspended Additional Superintendent of Police Nayini Bhujanga Rao in connection with a disproportionate assets case. He is also under investigation for alleged phone tapping.

articles.headline: Assembly election results reshape political landscape
articles.summary: Recent Assembly election results in Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Kerala, and Assam indicate significant shifts in the political landscape, with particular focus on unexpected outcomes in Tamil Nadu and West Bengal.

articles.headline: Delhi HC ruling on 'right to be forgotten' balances privacy and public interest
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court's decision on the 'right to be forgotten' underscores the tension between privacy and open justice. The ruling emphasizes the need for digital accuracy and contextual presentation of court records.

articles.headline: Focus shifts to Adivasi trust after Maoist challenge
articles.summary: The Union Home Minister stated that integrating Adivasis into the mainstream is key to lasting peace in Bastar. The article stresses the importance of earnestly implementing the PESA Act.

articles.headline: Startup India scheme boosts India's innovation ecosystem
articles.summary: The Startup India initiative has cultivated a supportive policy environment, driving robust growth and investment, making India a leading startup nation. The trend also shows a migration towards Tier 3 cities.

articles.headline: 3 arrested after 'Khan Sir' coaching institute vandalized in Patna
articles.summary: Three individuals, including a coaching institute director, were arrested after a vandalization incident at 'Khan Sir's' coaching centre in Patna. A security guard was injured, sparking student protests.

articles.headline: Judicial holidays are crucial working periods for judges
articles.summary: The article argues that judicial holidays are not merely rest periods but essential working windows for judges to finalize judgments, review cases, and prepare for hearings, highlighting the demanding nature of their roles.

articles.headline: Pawan Kalyan's Telangana political re-entry sparks debate
articles.summary: Pawan Kalyan's return to Telangana politics, marked by a controversial statement about YSRCP, has ignited a political discussion. The BJP is reportedly considering an alliance with his Jana Sena Party.

articles.headline: Uttar Pradesh Police's 'Operation Langda' raises due process concerns
articles.summary: The article questions the increasing use of 'Operation Langda' (leg-shooting) by UP Police as a crime-control strategy, raising concerns about due process, the rule of law, and systemic incentives.

articles.headline: Lack of local instrumentation hindering India's climate research
articles.summary: A report indicates that India's climate research is hampered by a lack of indigenous scientific instrument manufacturing capabilities, leading to reliance on imports and potential data inaccuracies. The Mega Science Vision initiative aims to address this.

articles.headline: Calcutta HC allows Abhishek Banerjee to file plea for protection
articles.summary: The Calcutta High Court permitted TMC leader Abhishek Banerjee to file a petition seeking protection against potential coercive action by the CID in a recruitment scam probe. The court will hear the matter soon.

articles.headline: Art reflects a world in transition at The Hindu Huddle
articles.summary: The Hindu Huddle 2026 will feature a panel discussing art's role in addressing global challenges like climate change and digital disruption, with participation from artists Seema Kohli and Shilo Shiv Suleman.

articles.headline: Congress leaders hail Karunanidhi on birth anniversary amid party strain
articles.summary: Rahul Gandhi and Mallikarjun Kharge paid tribute to M. Karunanidhi on his birth anniversary, aiming to ease tensions in the relationship between the Congress and DMK.

articles.headline: Trinamool loyalists reject ousted leader's claim to LoP post
articles.summary: Trinamool Congress loyalists have stated that an expelled leader cannot hold the position of Leader of the Opposition, emphasizing Mamata Banerjee as the party's sole leader.

articles.headline: National Conference to protest in Delhi for Statehood
articles.summary: Jammu and Kashmir's National Conference plans a protest in Delhi demanding Statehood and restoration of constitutional guarantees. A legislator also raised concerns about opacity in clearances for a Nicobar project.

articles.headline: CJP to protest on June 6, demands Education Minister's resignation
articles.summary: The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) will stage a protest in Delhi on June 6, demanding the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan over alleged incompetence and corruption.

articles.headline: Centre considering policies to boost E85 fuel adoption
articles.summary: The government is contemplating policies to promote E85 fuel (85% ethanol blend), which is expected to be cheaper and significantly reduce CO2 emissions, according to Union Minister Hardeep Singh Puri.

articles.headline: SBI preparing for second M&A deal this year
articles.summary: State Bank of India (SBI) Chairman C.S. Setty announced the bank is poised for a second M&A transaction this year, following the completion of its first, and is well-prepared with its systems and team.

articles.headline: RBI denies selling gold; reserves stable at 880.52 tonnes
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has refuted reports of selling $12 billion worth of gold to bolster foreign currency reserves. The central bank confirmed its gold reserves remain steady at 880.52 tonnes.

articles.headline: Sabalenka confused by open roof during French Open match loss
articles.summary: Aryna Sabalenka expressed bewilderment over the decision to keep the roof open during a windy match, which she believes contributed to her defeat against Diana Shnaider in the French Open quarterfinals.

articles.headline: Praggnanandhaa defeats Magnus Carlsen again in Norway Chess
articles.summary: R. Praggnanandhaa secured his second consecutive classical win against World No. 1 Magnus Carlsen at Norway Chess. This victory elevates Praggnanandhaa to third place in the standings.

articles.headline: Shnaider upsets Sabalenka; India to tour New Zealand
articles.summary: Diana Shnaider eliminated World No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka in the French Open quarterfinals. Separately, India is scheduled for a 10-white-ball series in New Zealand from October to December.

articles.headline: England wins T20 series against India
articles.summary: Alice Capsey's 82 and Heather Knight's unbeaten 70 led England to a six-wicket victory over India in the third T20I, clinching the series 2-1. The loss provides India with lessons for the upcoming T20 World Cup.

articles.headline: Abhinandh and Hansini win U-19 titles in Norway
articles.summary: P.B. Abhinandh and M. Hansini claimed the boys' and girls' under-19 singles titles, respectively, at the WTT Youth Contender in Sandefjord, Norway. Ayush Shetty progressed to the Indonesia Open pre-quarters.

articles.headline: Wales hockey veteran Sarah Jones reflects on career and future
articles.summary: Wales hockey player Sarah Jones reflects on her 15-year career, the sport's evolution, and her involvement in fencing, emphasizing inclusiveness and a focus on the present.

articles.headline: Exercise-induced liver protein reverses memory loss in mice
articles.summary: A study reveals that a liver protein, TNAP, linked to cognitive decline, can be reduced by exercise-induced GPLD1, reversing memory loss in mice and offering potential for Alzheimer's treatment.

articles.headline: Smallest liquid droplets observed behaving as fluid at LHC
articles.summary: Physicists at the LHC observed that a tiny droplet of quark-gluon plasma, created from oxygen ion collisions, acts as a fluid, pushing the boundaries of how small a liquid can be.

articles.headline: Iran strikes Kuwait airport after US targets Qeshm Island
articles.summary: Iranian drones attacked Kuwait's main airport, causing casualties. The US military confirmed targeting an Iranian ground control station on Qeshm Island in response.

articles.headline: Drone strike kills seven in Russia-held eastern Ukraine
articles.summary: A drone strike hit a bus in Russia-controlled eastern Ukraine's Donetsk region, killing seven people and injuring eleven. Russia also reported intercepting hundreds of Ukrainian drones.

articles.headline: Sri Lanka court bans ex-President Gotabaya from foreign travel
articles.summary: A Sri Lankan court has prohibited former President Gotabaya Rajapaksa and two military personnel from traveling abroad as part of investigations into the 2019 Easter Sunday terror attacks.

articles.headline: Former Nepal PM Oli denies territorial encroachment on India
articles.summary: Former Nepal PM K.P. Sharma Oli stated that Nepal has not encroached on Indian land and criticized the current PM's statements as 'traitorous' and potentially serving Washington's interests against China.

articles.headline: Brazil lawmakers vote to restrict abortion for underage rape victims
articles.summary: Brazilian lawmakers have approved measures to limit abortion access for underage rape victims, contradicting a prior resolution that prioritized victims' interests.

articles.headline: Violent protests erupt in Southampton after murder ruling
articles.summary: Protests turned violent in Southampton following a sentencing for murder, with demonstrators clashing with police. MPs are examining the role of race and policing in the case.

articles.headline: Biden admin seeks to secure US chip supply chain
articles.summary: The Biden administration is aiming to bolster the U.S. semiconductor supply chain through incentives for domestic manufacturing and research, reducing foreign dependency.

articles.headline: Indian tyre exports reach record ₹27,312 crore in FY26
articles.summary: Indian tyre exports hit an all-time high of ₹27,312 crore in FY2025-26, a 9% increase from the previous year, with the U.S. as the primary export market.

articles.headline: Wheat procurement surges 17% to 35 million tonnes
articles.summary: Government wheat procurement in the 2026-27 rabi season reached over 35 million tonnes, a 17% increase year-on-year, exceeding targets. Punjab and Madhya Pradesh led procurement efforts.

articles.headline: Fairwood, SK Securities pact for small modular reactors in India
articles.summary: Fairwood Nuclear Pvt. Ltd. (India) and SK Securities Co. Ltd. (South Korea) have signed a collaboration agreement to support the development and financing of small modular reactors (SMRs) in India.

articles.headline: North Korea criticizes US remarks on South Korea
articles.summary: North Korea condemned a US military official's comparison of South Korea to a 'dagger in the heart of Asia', viewing it as a strategy to contain China and reflecting a hostile stance.

articles.headline: Denmark PM presents new Cabinet with majority of women ministers
articles.summary: Denmark's Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen unveiled a new Cabinet consisting of 11 women out of 21 ministers, marking the first time women constitute a majority in the government.

articles.headline: Mizoram to issue liquor permits for medical reasons
articles.summary: Under its amended prohibition law, Mizoram will issue 'Red Card' permits for liquor consumption on medical grounds, valid for one year upon payment of ₹5,000.

articles.headline: Bengal's Annapurna Bhandar scheme to provide ₹3,000 monthly to 28 lakh women
articles.summary: West Bengal CM launched the Annapurna Bhandar scheme, offering ₹3,000 per month to eligible women aged 25-60. So far, 28,25,769 women have been approved for this financial aid.

articles.headline: Arunachal Christian body urges halt to anti-conversion law rules
articles.summary: The Arunachal Pradesh Christian Forum has asked the state government to pause rule-framing for the anti-conversion law and consult stakeholders, citing concerns about religious freedom.

articles.headline: Indian hockey teams dominate pool games, reach semifinals
articles.summary: The Indian men's hockey team beat Taiwan 13-1 to reach the U-18 Asia Cup semifinals, while the women's team topped their pool by defeating Singapore 25-0.

articles.headline: NHRC notices to Odisha, UP over tribal girl's assault
articles.summary: The National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) has issued notices to the governments of Odisha and Uttar Pradesh regarding the trafficking and repeated sexual assault of a 17-year-old tribal girl allegedly lured to UP for work.

articles.headline: ED searches linked to India-Cambodia trafficking syndicate
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) conducted searches in UP and Punjab in connection with a human trafficking syndicate suspected of cyber fraud and money laundering between India and Cambodia.

articles.headline: Gujarat detains 362 Bangladeshi immigrants in anti-infiltration drive
articles.summary: Gujarat Police detained 362 undocumented Bangladeshi immigrants and questioned over 782 suspected foreign nationals during 'Operation Delta Hunt', a state-wide crackdown.

articles.headline: Bangladeshi youth arrested in Agartala before Amit Shah's visit
articles.summary: Tripura Police arrested a Bangladeshi youth, Sajib Bhuyan, in Agartala ahead of Union Home Minister Amit Shah's visit. Bhuyan claimed he was fleeing political violence in Bangladesh.

articles.headline: Madras HC to hear plea on Tamil Nadu 'Pattali' caste reservation issue
articles.summary: The Madras High Court will hear a plea concerning reservation policies and their implementation related to the Pattali Makkal Katchi (PMK) party's advocacy for specific caste inclusions.

articles.headline: France fines fashion giant Shein over €22 million
articles.summary: French authorities have fined fashion retailer Shein more than €22 million for violations related to product traceability, environmental labeling, and delivery times.

articles.headline: Japan's fertility rate hits record low
articles.summary: Japan's fertility rate has fallen to a historic low, contributing to population decline, an ageing society, labor shortages, and increased social security costs.

articles.headline: India receives fourth Russian S-400 air defence squadron
articles.summary: India has received the fourth squadron of the Russian S-400 air defence system, part of a $5.43 billion deal. Deliveries of the fifth squadron are expected by year-end.

articles.headline: India to play 10 white-ball games in New Zealand series
articles.summary: India's tour of New Zealand will feature a 10-white-ball series from October 22 to December 1, including five T20Is and five ODIs, marking the largest bilateral series held in New Zealand.

articles.headline: Manav Suthar, Harsh Dubey vie for Test spot in Jadeja's absence
articles.summary: With Ravindra Jadeja rested, left-arm spinners Manav Suthar and Harsh Dubey are contenders for a Test call-up, potentially becoming long-term successors.

articles.headline: Rooftop solar adoption stalls due to QC confusion
articles.summary: Confusion over quality control standards is delaying the adoption of rooftop solar systems, hindering expansion plans and consumer benefits.

articles.headline: Modi to become longest-serving elected PM on June 10
articles.summary: Narendra Modi is set to become India's longest-serving elected head of government on June 10, marking a significant tenure of leadership.

articles.headline: Govt allocates ₹10,000 cr to cap jet fuel prices
articles.summary: The government has earmarked ₹10,000 crore to stabilize jet fuel prices, aiming to control airfare costs and provide relief to airlines, especially amidst the West Asia crisis.

articles.headline: New Zealand's Sarpreet Singh embodies Sikh pride at World Cup
articles.summary: New Zealand player Sarpreet Singh is being celebrated for his Sikh identity during the World Cup, resonating with fans globally.

articles.headline: Ukraine strikes St Petersburg oil depot and naval base
articles.summary: Ukrainian drones targeted an oil terminal and a naval base in St Petersburg shortly before President Putin's economic forum, an act seen as an attempt to embarrass the Kremlin.

articles.headline: DK Shivakumar sworn in as Karnataka CM; Parameshwara deputy
articles.summary: D.K. Shivakumar took oath as Karnataka CM, with G. Parameshwara as deputy CM and a 12-member cabinet. Initiatives like free bus passes for students were announced.

articles.headline: Cabinet approves ₹10,000 crore fund for jet fuel price stabilization
articles.summary: The Union Cabinet sanctioned a ₹10,000 crore interest-free advance fund to Oil Marketing Companies (OMCs) to stabilize Aviation Turbine Fuel (ATF) prices for Indian airlines over 36 months.

articles.headline: Unnao hospital owner arrested for murder after harassment complaint
articles.summary: Devkant Uttam, owner of Unnao's Uttam Hospital, and his nephew were arrested for abducting and murdering a woman who had filed a sexual harassment complaint against Uttam two days prior. Her body was found in Bulandshahr.

articles.headline: Suryakumar Yadav likely dropped from T20 team; Iyer considered for captaincy
articles.summary: Suryakumar Yadav's poor form may lead to his exclusion from India's T20 World Cup squad, with Shreyas Iyer reportedly being considered for the No. 4 position and captaincy.

articles.headline: India's Services PMI rises in May, highest since November
articles.summary: India's services sector expanded significantly in May, with the HSBC India Services PMI reaching 59.8, driven by increased demand and new business, while export activity also grew.

articles.headline: Government considering tax exemption for foreign G-sec holdings
articles.summary: The Indian government is contemplating removing capital gains tax on foreign investments in government securities (G-secs) to attract foreign capital and enhance liquidity in the debt market.

articles.headline: US considers 12.5% duty on Indian goods over Section 301 probes
articles.summary: The US is reportedly considering a 12.5% duty on Indian goods as part of its Section 301 investigation. India is engaged in talks with Washington to resolve these trade concerns.

articles.headline: Indian firms reportedly gain access to advanced AI platform 'Claude Mythos'
articles.summary: Several Indian companies are reportedly gaining access to 'Claude Mythos', a platform offering advanced AI capabilities, signaling India's increasing engagement with cutting-edge technologies.

articles.headline: Siddaramaiah reportedly pushed for Chief Minister position
articles.summary: Sources suggest Siddaramaiah was instrumental in securing the Chief Minister's post through lobbying and strategic political maneuvering.
